element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player selects from different elemental weapons affecting strategy
Escalating complexity across selectable level sequence

typical_tension_triggers: Multi-directional attack patterns requiring up/down response
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Continuous vertical autoscroll creates steady pressure; intensity modulates via enemy density and attack patterns rather than pause opportunities

difficulty_curve.early_game: Easy - described as having gentle difficulty by MegaTech and Console XS reviewers
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate - player-selectable level order allows customization of difficulty progression
difficulty_curve.late_game: Escalates through fixed final three levels with intensified enemy patterns
notable_spikes: Transition from levels 1-4 (player-chosen, forgiving) to levels 5-7 (fixed, unavoidable)
Final boss difficulty spike - Gyra's forces and Roki encounter

notable_level_patterns.description: First four levels can be tackled in any order, allowing skill-based progression customization before linear endgame
Constant upward scroll creates momentum; player controls lateral positioning and directional fire (up/down) but cannot control vertical pace
Different magic types serve as loadout choices affecting playstyle; sources note exceptional level design supporting varied approaches
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Weapon selection tied to character progression

sources_used: wikipedia - Elemental Master article (gameplay, plot, development, reception details)
